nodes.label:
Water
1x / day
nodes.desc: Minimal watering. Good for the water table, but risks hydric stress for the plant.

nodes.label:
Water
2x / day
nodes.desc: The horticultural standard. Ensures stable growth but starts to weigh on water resources.

nodes.label:
Water
3x / day
nodes.desc: Intensive watering. Aims for maximum yield at the expense of evaporation and soil leaching.

nodes.label: Save water
nodes.desc: The conviction that fresh water is scarce. The more we water (A2, A3), the more this score collapses.

nodes.label:
Preserve
soil quality
nodes.desc: Excessive watering (A3) leaches nutrients and compacts the soil. Bad for the ecosystem long-term.

nodes.label:
Maximise
harvest
nodes.desc: The goal is to produce as much as possible. Attacked by water scarcity (A1) or a too-radical sobriety ethic (B_ENV_EAU). Supported by moderate watering (A2).

nodes.label:
Minimise
operating costs
nodes.desc: Water cost is an operating expense. Intensive watering directly reduces profitability.
